q: I won 18 major championships, more than any golfer in history. Who am I?
Called 'The King', this golfer helped popularize the sport on television and built a massive fan base known as 'Arnie's Army.' Name him.
This golfer won the 2000 U.S. Open at Pebble Beach by a record 15 strokes. Name him.
I won the career Grand Slam before age 25 and returned from spinal fusion surgery to win the 2019 Masters. Who am I?
Called 'The Golden Bear', this golfer dominated the majors across four decades. Name him.

This golfer chipped in on the 16th hole at Augusta National during the final round of the 2005 Masters, producing one of golf's most iconic shots as the ball paused on the lip before dropping. Name him.
He won 82 PGA Tour events, tying Sam Snead's all-time record. Who is he?
This golfer holds the record for most consecutive cuts made on the PGA Tour with 142. Name him.
Known as 'The Black Knight', this South African won nine major championships and is one of only five golfers to complete the career Grand Slam. Name him.
I am the only golfer to win all four major championships in the same calendar year, accomplishing the feat in 1930 before retiring at age 28. Who am I?
This golfer won 11 consecutive PGA Tour tournaments in 1945, a streak that remains the longest in tour history. Name him.
He won three major championships in a single season after a near-fatal car accident, capturing the 1953 Masters, U.S. Open, and Open Championship. Who was he?
This Spanish golfer won five major championships and was known for his swashbuckling style, incredible short game, and thrilling Ryder Cup performances. Name him.
Called 'Lefty', this golfer won six major championships including the 2021 PGA Championship at age 50, becoming the oldest major champion in modern history. Name him.
This Northern Irish golfer won the U.S. Open by eight strokes at Congressional Country Club at age 22. Name him.
He won back-to-back U.S. Opens in 2017 and 2018 and back-to-back PGA Championships in 2018 and 2019, amassing four majors before age 30. Who is he?
This golfer won the Masters in both 2022 and 2024, quickly establishing himself as the world's top-ranked player. Name him.
I won six Open Championships, more than any other golfer in history, and have a trophy named after me that is awarded at each Open. Who am I?
The famous par-3 12th hole at Augusta National, known as Golden Bell, plays over this body of water. Name it.
This par-3 island green hole at TPC Sawgrass is one of the most famous holes in golf. What is its number?
He holed a 4-wood for a double eagle on the 15th hole at Augusta National in the 1935 Masters, a shot often called 'the shot heard round the world.' Who was he?
Tom Watson nearly won a record-tying sixth Open Championship at this links course in 2009 at age 59, losing in a playoff to Stewart Cink. Name the course.
This golfer won the 1986 Masters at age 46, shooting a final-round 65 to claim his 18th and final major championship. Name him.
He won 18 PGA Tour events in a single season in 1945, a record that still stands. Who was he?
The Ryder Cup pits a team from the United States against a team from where?
I became the youngest Masters champion in history when I won the green jacket at age 21 in 1997. Who am I?
This golfer famously made 'The Concession' at the 1969 Ryder Cup, conceding a short putt to Jack Nicklaus on the final hole to halve their singles match and tie the overall competition. Name him.
He holds the record for the largest margin of victory in Masters history, winning by 12 strokes in 1997. Who is he?
The Road Hole bunker is a famous hazard on the 17th hole at which legendary links course?
Called 'The Hawk', this golfer was known for his relentless ball-striking precision and won nine major championships. Name him.
This South African golfer shot 62 at the 2017 Open Championship at Royal Birkdale, the first score of 62 or better in men's major championship history. Name him.
He won two consecutive U.S. Open titles in 2017 and 2018, becoming the first golfer to do so since Curtis Strange. Who is he?
Amen Corner at Augusta National consists of the second half of the 11th hole, all of the 12th hole, and the first half of which hole?
Bobby Jones co-founded Augusta National Golf Club and helped create the Masters Tournament with this club chairman. Name the chairman.
This golfer won the 2016 Open Championship at Royal Troon in a historic duel with Phil Mickelson, finishing at 20-under par. Name him.
He won five PGA Championships, more than any other golfer in the stroke-play era. Who is he?
Called 'The Big Easy' for his smooth, effortless swing, this South African won four major championships. Name him.
This Fijian golfer won three major championships, including back-to-back PGA Tour money titles in 2003 and 2004. Name him.
I am the only golfer to hold all four professional major championship trophies at the same time, a feat called the 'Tiger Slam.' Who am I?
This Scottish golfer became the first British player to win the Masters when he captured the green jacket in 1988. Name him.
He won six Masters titles, more than any other golfer in history. Who is he?
The Swilcan Bridge is a famous landmark on the 18th hole of which iconic golf course?
This Englishman won three Open Championships and three Masters titles, totaling six major victories, and was known for his meticulous course management. Name him.
This Australian became the first from his country to win the Masters when he captured the green jacket in 2013. Name him.
Known as 'The Great White Shark', this Australian golfer was world number one for 331 weeks but is remembered for heartbreaking major losses. Name him.
This course, located in Georgia, hosts the Masters Tournament every year and is famous for its azaleas and Magnolia Lane. Name it.
He hit one of golf's most famous shots, a 1-iron to the 18th green at Merion in the final round of the 1950 U.S. Open, just 16 months after a near-fatal car accident. Who was he?
This left-handed golfer from New Zealand was the first lefty to win a major championship, taking the 1963 Open Championship. Name him.
He won the 2012 Masters by holing a miraculous hook shot from deep in the trees on the second playoff hole. Who is he?
Tom Watson won five Open Championships. At which course did he win his most famous, beating Jack Nicklaus in the 'Duel in the Sun' in 1977?
This golfer won the 2020 Masters in November (delayed due to COVID-19), finishing at a record 20-under par. Name him.
He won the 2021 U.S. Open at Torrey Pines for his first major championship, finishing one stroke ahead of Louis Oosthuizen. Who is he?
He famously blew a six-stroke lead on the final day of the 1996 Masters, losing to Nick Faldo by five strokes. Who was he?
This golfer won the 2020 U.S. Open at Winged Foot, overpowering the course after a dramatic physical transformation. Name him.
He won the 2020 PGA Championship in his first ever PGA Championship start at TPC Harding Park. Who is he?
Known as 'Slammin' Sam', this golfer won 82 PGA Tour events but never won the U.S. Open despite finishing runner-up four times. Name him.
The Solheim Cup is the women's equivalent of the Ryder Cup. It pits the United States against a team from where?
This German golfer won the Masters twice (1985 and 1993) and became one of Europe's most successful Ryder Cup captains. Name him.
The 'Church Pews' bunker is a famous hazard found between the 3rd and 4th holes at which major championship venue?
He won the 2015 Open Championship at St Andrews in a playoff, his second major title. Who is he?
Jim Furyk holds the record for the lowest single-round score in PGA Tour history. What was that score?
Arnold Palmer charged from seven strokes back in the final round to win the 1960 U.S. Open at this course. Name the course.
This golfer won the 2010 Open Championship at St Andrews by seven strokes in his first major victory. Name him.
He won the 1995 Open Championship at St Andrews, one of his two major titles, and was known for his wild, grip-it-and-rip-it style. Who was he?
The par-5 13th hole at Augusta National, called Azalea, is part of Amen Corner. What type of dogleg is it?
He made the clinching putt for Europe at the 2012 Ryder Cup at Medinah, completing the 'Miracle at Medinah' comeback from a 10-6 deficit. Who was he?
Called 'The Postman' for always delivering at the Ryder Cup, this Englishman is famous for his intense match-play performances for Europe. Name him.
This golfer won the 2013 U.S. Open at Merion and the 2016 Olympic gold medal in golf's return to the Games. Name him.
Walter Hagen won five PGA Championships, all in the match-play format. How many of those five were consecutive?
I won the 2015 Masters and the 2015 U.S. Open in the same year and nearly won the calendar-year Grand Slam. Who am I?
This golfer won the 2011 Masters by birdieing the final four holes of regulation to beat Jason Day and Adam Scott by two strokes. Name him.
He won 15 major championships, second only to Jack Nicklaus on the all-time list. Who is he?
Called 'El Nino', this Spanish golfer won the 2017 Masters for his first major title after years of near-misses. Name him.
This course, nicknamed 'The Monster', hosted the 2002 and 2008 U.S. Opens and is located on Long Island. Name it.
He entered the 1991 PGA Championship at Crooked Stick as a ninth alternate and stunned the golf world by winning it. Who was he?
This golfer famously holed a bunker shot on the 18th hole to win the 1986 PGA Championship, beating Greg Norman. Name him.
He chipped in on the second playoff hole to win the 1987 Masters, breaking Greg Norman's heart again. Who was he?
The 'Bear Trap' is a notoriously difficult three-hole stretch (15th through 17th) at which PGA Tour venue?
